lede: A look at March 11 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Margarita Kolosov and Dario Cologna, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Tatmadaw
events.desc: The Burmese military kills at least 30 villagers, including 3 Buddhist monks, during the Pinlaung massacre in Shan State, Myanmar.

events.title: World Health Organization
events.desc: The World Health Organization (WHO) declares the COVID-19 virus epidemic a pandemic.

events.title: 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami
events.desc: An earthquake measuring 9.0 in magnitude strikes 130 km (81 mi) east of Sendai, Japan, triggering a tsunami killing thousands of people. This event also triggered the second largest nuclear accident in history, and one of only two events to be classified as a Level 7 on the International Nuclear Event Scale.

events.title: Sebastián Piñera
events.desc: Economist and businessman Sebastián Piñera is sworn in as President of Chile. Aftershocks of the 2010 Pichilemu earthquakes hit central Chile during the ceremony.

events.title: 2009 Winnenden shootings
events.desc: Winnenden school shooting: Fifteen are killed and nine are injured before recent graduate Tim Kretschmer shoots and kills himself, leading to tightened weapons restrictions in Germany.

events.title: Coastal road massacre
events.desc: Coastal Road massacre: At least 37 are killed and more than 70 are wounded when Fatah hijack an Israeli bus, prompting Israel's Operation Litani.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: United States President Franklin D. Roosevelt signs the Lend-Lease Act into law, allowing American-built war supplies to be shipped to the Allies on loan.

events.title: Great Blizzard of 1888
events.desc: The Great Blizzard of 1888 begins along the eastern seaboard of the United States, shutting down commerce and killing more than 400 people.

events.title: Rigoletto
events.desc: The first performance of Rigoletto by Giuseppe Verdi takes place in Venice.

events.title: Anne, Queen of Great Britain
events.desc: Queen Anne withholds Royal Assent from the Scottish Militia Bill, the last time a British monarch vetoes legislation.

deaths.text: Junior Bridgeman, American basketball player and businessman
F. W. Murnau, German-American director, producer, and screenwriter

observances.text: Day of Restoration of Independence from the Soviet Union in 1990 (Lithuania)
